learn.title: How Wind Turbines Turn Wind into Power
learn.intro: Wind energy is a cornerstone of the global transition to clean power. By capturing the kinetic energy of moving air, we can generate massive amounts of electricity without burning fossil fuels. Understanding how these giant structures function is the first step in appreciating how a simple breeze can power an entire city sustainably.
learn.c2_text: The blades are shaped like airplane wings. As wind flows over them, it creates a pressure difference known as lift, which pulls the blade forward, causing the rotor to spin.
learn.c3_text: The spinning rotor turns a shaft connected to a generator inside the nacelle. This movement spins magnets near copper coils, converting mechanical energy into electricity for the grid.
learn.stat1: Wind energy provides clean, renewable electricity
learn.stat2: Kinetic energy converts to electrical energy

learn.title: Climate Finance and Global Energy Equity
learn.intro: The transition to net-zero requires massive capital shifts. For developing nations, access to climate finance is the difference between fossil fuel dependency and a clean energy future. International agreements aim to bridge this gap, ensuring that the global move toward renewables is both rapid and equitable for all nations.
learn.c2_text: Finance flows are essential to de-risk renewable projects in emerging markets, making it easier for investors to fund large-scale solar and wind farms in developing regions.
learn.c3_text: Ensuring developing nations have the capital to bypass coal-fired power plants is vital for global climate goals and stabilizing the international energy market.
learn.stat1: Global energy outlook requires massive investment
learn.stat4: Equity is key to net-zero goals

learn.title: The Power of Offshore Wind Farms
learn.intro: Offshore wind offers a massive opportunity for high-capacity power generation. By placing turbines in the ocean, we tap into stronger, more consistent winds than those found on land, making it a critical component for large-scale decarbonization of coastal industrial hubs.
learn.c2_text: Ocean winds are much more consistent than land winds. Think of it like a steady stream versus a flickering candle; the steady ocean wind keeps the turbines spinning more reliably.
learn.c3_text: Connecting these farms to land requires subsea cables. In India, offshore wind projects are being explored along the coasts of Gujarat and Tamil Nadu.
learn.stat1: Offshore winds are stronger and steadier

learn.title: Decoding the Hydrogen Color Code
learn.intro: Hydrogen is a versatile energy carrier, but not all hydrogen is created equal. As we decarbonize, the method used to produce hydrogen becomes crucial. Understanding the 'color' of hydrogen helps us distinguish between fuels that help the planet and those that contribute to global warming.
learn.c2_text: Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis using renewable energy like solar or wind. It is the 'gold standard' because its only byproduct is pure water, making it carbon-neutral.
learn.c3_text: Grey hydrogen is made from natural gas without capturing the carbon. This is currently the most common method but contributes to greenhouse gas emissions.
learn.stat3: Grey hydrogen emits CO2 into air
learn.stat4: Hydrogen is a versatile energy carrier

learn.title: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission
learn.intro: India is positioning itself as a global leader in the hydrogen economy. The National Green Hydrogen Mission is a strategic roadmap designed to create a massive ecosystem for green hydrogen production, driving down costs and ensuring energy security for the nation.
learn.c2_text: The mission aims to make India a major hub for green hydrogen production. This involves scaling up electrolyzer manufacturing to drive down the cost per kilogram.
learn.c3_text: The Indian government provides SIGHT incentives to encourage domestic manufacturing and large-scale deployment of green hydrogen technologies.

learn.title: Floating Solar: Energy on Water
learn.intro: Floating solar (floatovoltaics) is a brilliant solution to land scarcity. By placing solar panels on reservoirs or lakes, we can generate clean power while simultaneously protecting our precious land resources and improving water conservation efforts.
learn.c2_text: Floating solar avoids using valuable agricultural or residential land. It is like using a floating raft to hold your solar panels instead of taking up space on the ground.
learn.c3_text: The shade provided by the panels reduces water evaporation from reservoirs, which is vital for water management in many regions.
learn.stat2: Water cools panels for higher efficiency
